data.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation in the repository.

### AC1 — All broad-except sites meet the observability floor
**MET.** Every `except Exception` site in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` carries a `# noqa: BLE001` with a justification reason string. 29 sites verified across `sidecar.py`, `registry.py`, `__init__.py`, `det_floor.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `attest.py`, and `sizing.py`. Sites annotated "broad-but-logged" also have a corresponding `logger.warning(..., exc_info=True)` call. No unjustified `except ...: pass` remains.

### AC2 — Stderr-diagnostic prints migrated to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`
**MET (vacuously).** `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` contains zero `print(` calls and zero `file=sys.stderr` references — the package was already fully logger-based. `logging.getLogger(__name__)` is defined in 5 modules (`sidecar.py`, `__init__.py`, `det_floor.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `attest.py`). The landing comment explicitly records this non-applicability.

### AC3 — `pyproject.toml` enables BLE001+T201 with a shrinking per-file-ignores allowlist
**MET.** `pyproject.toml` line 184: `select = ["E", "F", "W", "I", "UP", "B", "BLE001", "T201"]`. A complete `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` table (lines 202–234) exempts all un-swept packages: `__init__.py`, `reducer/*`, `graph/*`, `_store/*`, `_io/*` (T201 only; BLE001 sites carry inline noqas), `grounding/*`, `_engine_support/*`, `mcp_server.py`, `llm/` top-level files (enumerated explicitly), `_cli/*`, `_commands/*`, `_engine/rebar_reconciler/*`, `_engine/jira-capability-probe.py`, and `tests/**`.

### AC4 — `llm/plan_review/` excluded from the allowlist
**MET.** `plan_review` does not appear anywhere in `pyproject.toml`. Lines 219–221 explicitly explain why: ruff per-file-ignore globs match `*` across `/`, so llm top-level files are enumerated individually to ensure `plan_review/` stays gated. The proving test `tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py` (all three test functions) directly validates this: `test_exemplar_path_is_gated` asserts BLE001+T201 fire on `plan_review/` paths, `test_allowlisted_path_is_suppressed` confirms the allowlist works elsewhere, and `test_exemplar_package_is_clean` verifies the real package is already conformant.

### AC5 — `make lint` + full test suite pass
**MET per evidence.** The landing comment (commit 46d1abee) records: "make lint green; full suite 3204 passed/12 skipped." The proving test file `tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py` exists and is non-trivial (3 live test functions using `subprocess.run` + ruff `--stdin-filename` to gate BLE001/T201 behaviour by path).

**Verdict: PASS** — every acceptance criterion stated by the ticket is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.
